sanskrit:
धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम्।
तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते॥
transliteration:
dhūmo rātristathā kṛṣṇaḥ ṣaṇ-māsā dakṣiṇāyanam |
tatra cāndramasaṃ jyotir yogī prāpya nivartate ||
literal_meaning: Smoke, night, the dark-fortnight, the six months of the sun’s southward course—there, having reached the lunar light, the yogī returns.
contextual_meaning: Paths of departure map onto inner lunar cycles: outward success that feels complete still waxes and wanes; the psyche that has not gone beyond the mind’s reflected light must recycle through the same emotional phases.

shadow: Unacknowledged grief over having to start again

psychological_state: Post-achievement plateau—visible milestones reached, yet an intuition that the same curriculum will reappear in a new form.
trigger_scenario: You finally get the promotion, publish the paper, or finish the marathon, celebrate under the full moon, and within weeks feel the old restlessness knocking.
anchor_text: True freedom ends the mind's lunar cycle of return.
